title: Build-context overlay in v1.44 breaks automatic fuse-overlayfs fallback
body:
### Issue Description

With buildah v1.43.1, if I have `storage.driver = "overlay"`, no `mount_program` configured, and the storage graph root doesn't support overlay, buildah automatically falls back to `fuse-overlayfs`.

Buildah v1.44.0 doesn't do that specifically when creating the overlay over the build context directory. It looks like the code for that doesn't have the same fallback behavior as the storage driver code.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Create a Containerfile for installing buildah + dependencies:

    ```Dockerfile
    FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi10/go-toolset:1.26.3 AS go-build

    ARG BUILDAH_TAG=v1.44.0

    USER 0

    # Install dependencies for compiling buildah
    RUN dnf -y install \
            bzip2 \
            glib2-devel \
            gpgme-devel \
            libassuan-devel \
            libseccomp-devel

    WORKDIR /build

    RUN git clone --branch=$BUILDAH_TAG https://github.com/podman-container-tools/buildah && \
        cd buildah && \
        make bin/buildah


    FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi10/ubi-minimal:10.1

    USER 0

    RUN microdnf -y install \
            fuse-overlayfs \
            containers-common-extra \
            gpgme \
            libassuan \
            libseccomp

    COPY --from=go-build /build/buildah/bin/buildah /usr/local/bin/buildah

    COPY <<EOF /etc/containers/storage.conf
    [storage]
    driver = "overlay"
    EOF

    ENV BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot
    ```

2. Build buildah 1.43.1 and 1.44.0:

    ```bash
    buildah build -t buildah:1.43.1 --build-arg BUILDAH_TAG=v1.43.1 .
    buildah build -t buildah:1.44.0 --build-arg BUILDAH_TAG=v1.44.0 .
    ```

3. Verify that automatic fuse-overlayfs fallback worked in v1.43.1:

    ```bash
    podman run --rm -i --device /dev/fuse buildah:1.43.1 bash <<EOF
    mkdir /tmp/buildcontext
    cd /tmp/buildcontext
    echo 'FROM scratch' > Containerfile
    buildah build
    EOF
    ```

4. Reproduce the failure in v1.44.0:

    ```bash
    podman run --rm -i --device /dev/fuse buildah:1.44.0 bash <<EOF
    mkdir /tmp/buildcontext
    cd /tmp/buildcontext
    echo 'FROM scratch' > Containerfile
    buildah build
    EOF
    ```

    ```text
    Error: mounting an overlay over build context directory: creating overlay scaffolding for build context directory: mount overlay:/var/tmp/buildah-context-3269457104/overlay/1341278794/merge, data: lowerdir=/tmp/buildcontext,upperdir=/var/tmp/buildah-context-3269457104/overlay/1341278794/upper,workdir=/var/tmp/buildah-context-3269457104/overlay/1341278794/work,userxattr: invalid argument
    ```

Note that adding `--volume /var/tmp` makes the build work in v1.44.0; my host filesystem is btrfs which supports native overlay. I believe this confirms that the issue is isolated to the build context overlay, mounting over `/var/lib/containers/storage` still falls back as expected.

Having to mount `/var/tmp` is also new in v1.44, but not entirely related. Reported as a separate issue: #6892


### Describe the results you received

Build-context overlay doesn't fall back to fuse-overlayfs, storage overlay does.

### Describe the results you expected

Build-context overlay would also fall back to fuse-overlayfs

### buildah version output

```yaml
Version:         1.44.0
Go Version:      go1.26.3 (Red Hat 1.26.3-4.el10_2)
Image Spec:      1.1.1
Runtime Spec:    1.3.0
image Version:   5.40.0
Git Commit:      30a4189415e5d0a3a0c5971307a6432d8ed1a097
Built:           Wed Jun  3 12:16:13 2026
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

```yaml
        "variant": ""
    },
    "store": {
        "ContainerStore": {

        },
        "GraphDriverName": "overlay",

        "GraphRoot": "/var/lib/containers/storage",
        "GraphStatus": {

            "Supports d_type": "true",

        "RunRoot": "/run/containers/storage"
    }

```

### Provide your storage.conf

```toml
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
```

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
